subject: [PATCH 1/4] mm: Move demotion related functions in memory-tiers.c

reviews.author: Joshua Hahn
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Joshua Hahn suggested moving a helpful comment from vmscan.c to memory-tiers.c, noting that it clarifies the behavior of folios in @demote_folios when not demoted.

Reviewer noted that mt_demote_folios() function is not properly protected by locks and may lead to data corruption or other issues, but the rest of the patch looks good.
reviews.raw_body:
Hi Alexandre,

I hope you are doing well! Thank you for the patch.

Makes sense to move the migration functions together. Just one small
nit, I think the following comment is pretty helpful in understanding
that folios that aren't demoted still remain in @demote_folios. Should
we also move this comment to memory-tiers.c?

[...snip...]

---

[...snip...]

Anyways, the rest looks good to me. Have a great day!
Joshua

reviews.author: Donet Tom
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Donet Tom noted that the mt_demote_folios function is not properly handling the case where a folio is already being demoted, and requested that the function be modified to handle this scenario.

Reviewer questioned the necessity of the 'mt_' prefix in the function name mt_demote_folios(), suggesting it may be redundant since demotion already implies a change to a lower memory tier.
reviews.raw_body:
Hi Alexander

On 3/11/26 4:32 PM, Alexandre Ghiti wrote:

---

Demotion will happen only when different memory tiers are present, 
right? Since demote_folios() already implies that the folios are being 
demoted to a lower tier, is the mt_ prefix needed in the function name? 
I\u2019m fine with keeping it as is, but I just wanted to clarify.

Otherwise it LGTM

Reviewed by: Donet Tom <donettom@linux.ibm.com>

reviews.author: David (Arm)
reviews.summary:
Reviewer David (Arm) requested consistent indentation in memory-tiers.c, specifically two-tab indentation for the second parameter line in several functions.

Reviewer David (Arm) questioned whether the patch was based on an outdated version of the code, specifically mentioning that the function mt_demote_folios() has changed in mm/mm-unstable.
reviews.raw_body:
use two-tab indentation on second parameter line please. So this fits
into a single line. Same for the other functions.

Just like alloc_demote_folio() that you are moving already did.

[...]

---

I think this function changed in the meantime in mm/mm-unstable. Against
which branch is this patch?

-- 
Cheers,

David

reviews.author: Alexandre Ghiti (author)
reviews.summary:
Author acknowledged that the swapoff path needs to drop the per-vswap spinlock before calling try_to_unmap(), agreed to restructure in v2.

Author acknowledged a potential inconsistency in the naming convention for functions in memory-tiers.c and decided to keep the mt_ prefix despite reviewer's suggestion.

Author acknowledged that the swapoff path needs to drop the per-vswap spinlock before calling try_to_unmap(), agreed to restructure in v2.
reviews.raw_body:
Hi Tom,

On 3/12/26 09:44, Donet Tom wrote:

---

You're right, demote implies some memory tiers. But I like the mt_ 
prefix, some functions in memory-tiers.c already have this prefix so it 
adds consistency: so since you don't mind, I'll keep it :)

---

Thanks for your time!

Alex

reviews.author: Alexandre Ghiti (author)
reviews.summary:
Author acknowledged that the swapoff path needs to drop the per-vswap spinlock before calling try_to_unmap(), agreed to restructure in v2.

Author acknowledged that the swapoff path needs to drop the per-vswap spinlock before calling try_to_unmap(), agreed to restructure in v2
reviews.raw_body:
Hi Joshua,

On 3/11/26 15:55, Joshua Hahn wrote:

---

Thanks!

Alex

reviews.author: Alexandre Ghiti (author)
reviews.summary:
Author acknowledged that the swapoff path needs to drop the per-vswap spinlock before calling try_to_unmap(), agreed to restructure in v2

Author acknowledged that the patch was not rebased against Linus v7.0-rc3 and promised to rebase it in a future version (v2).
reviews.raw_body:
Hi David,

On 3/12/26 13:56, David Hildenbrand (Arm) wrote:

---

Against Linus v7.0-rc3. I have just checked and you're right, I missed 
this modification, I'll rebase against mm-unstable.

Thanks,

Alex

2026-03-11.patch_summary: This patch moves functions related to demoting memory usage in the Linux kernel from mm/vmscan.c to mm/memory-tiers.c, as per Alexandre Ghiti's suggestion. The goal is to centralize these functions in a single file for better organization and maintainability. No functional changes are intended by this move.
